name: OTTO BREMER TRUST
reasoning: Otto Bremer Trust explicitly funds only in Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, and Wisconsin — VetsBoats is in California and would not qualify under any geographic criteria. The vet grants in their history are all to MN-based organizations.

name: PARK FOUNDATION INC
reasoning: Park Foundation funds environment, civic participation, media, and democracy — adaptive sailing for veterans has zero overlap with any of these program areas. The single vet grant was for public lands advocacy, not therapeutic recreation.

name: PATRICK & SHIRLEY CAMPBELL FOUNDATION
reasoning: The foundation's website redirected to a California business directory (cacompanyregistry.com), yielding no useful eligibility information. Assets of $942K and annual giving of $28,839 make this too small to be meaningful; vet grants were symbolic $100-$500 donations to national organizations.

name: Philanthropy International
reasoning: Philanthropy International is a fundraising and financial planning service for charities — not a traditional grantmaker accepting applications from nonprofits. Their 'grants' to vet organizations (Pat Tillman Foundation) appear to be donor-advised fund distributions or planned giving vehicles, not open grants. They do not have an application process for nonprofits seeking funding.

name: RASMUSON FOUNDATION
reasoning: Rasmuson Foundation's explicit mission is 'to empower Alaskans to help each other' — all grants go to Alaska nonprofits, tribes, and local governments. VetsBoats is in California and would be ineligible regardless of mission fit.

name: RB AND TWYLA SHELDAHL FOUNDATION
reasoning: The foundation's website redirected to TaxExemptWorld, a 990 lookup database — no application information available. Reported assets of $0 with a single vet grant ($10,000 to Tunnels to Towers) suggests this is a small pass-through or deceased foundation with no application process.

name: RICHARD KING MELLON FOUNDATION
reasoning: Richard King Mellon Foundation funds Southwestern Pennsylvania exclusively (conservation, economic development, health, economic mobility in Pittsburgh region). VetsBoats is in California; there is no geographic overlap and no CA grantmaking.

name: RICHARD LOUNSBERY FOUNDATION INC
reasoning: The Richard Lounsbery Foundation explicitly states it 'rarely accepts unsolicited proposals' and lists 'Unsolicited proposals' as a category it does not fund. Their mission is science/technology, French-American cooperation, and science education — zero overlap with adaptive sailing or veteran recreation.

name: RICHARD REED FOUNDATION
reasoning: Richard Reed Foundation funds human support (mental/physical health), animal support (equine therapy), and environmental causes — they have funded equine therapy for veterans. Their current grant cycle is open (Jan 15-Mar 15, 2026 and June 15-Aug 15, 2026) and they accept LOIs. No explicit PF exclusion found; the adaptive recreation/therapeutic aspect aligns with their human+mental health focus. National scope apparent. However, no direct sailing/water sports precedent and PF eligibility is unconfirmed.

name: ROBERT & JOAN DIRCKS FOUNDATION INC
reasoning: Dircks Foundation explicitly states it serves 'primarily New Jersey area' and 'locations of Foundation Trustees.' VetsBoats is in San Francisco Bay Area. Their vet grants were small ($2,500-$5,000) to homeless veteran shelters and service dog programs — no adaptive recreation focus.

name: ROBERT & TONI BADER CHARITABLE
reasoning: Robert & Toni Bader Charitable Foundation explicitly funds only '501(c)3 organizations' and focuses on Jewish ideals through science, education, and the arts. VetsBoats is a private foundation (not 501c3 public charity) and adaptive sailing has no overlap with their stated Jewish ideals/science/arts mission.

name: ROCKEFELLER BROTHERS FUND INC
reasoning: Rockefeller Brothers Fund focuses on democratic practice, sustainable development, peacebuilding, and arts/culture in specific pivotal places (Central America, China, Western Balkans). Their single vet grant was for anti-war advocacy networks. No application process found for external nonprofits; grantmaking appears invitation-only and strategy-driven.

name: RUSSELL SAGE FOUNDATION
reasoning: Russell Sage Foundation funds social science research — it supports academic scholars and research projects, not program delivery organizations. Their grants go to universities and researchers. VetsBoats runs programs, not research, making this a complete mission mismatch.

name: RightSpace Storage Foundation
reasoning: RightSpace Storage Foundation is a corporate giving arm that directs funds to pre-selected charitable organizations (Wounded Warrior, Gary Sinise, Tunnels to Towers) — all large national veterans nonprofits. There is no application process for new grantees; they self-direct 10% of revenue to chosen partners. Assets of $114K and $102K annual giving also make this too small for meaningful grants.

name: SPECTEMUR AGENDO INC
reasoning: Spectemur Agendo (Stewart R. Mott Foundation) has officially closed as of June 30, 2022 and is no longer accepting any applications or making grants. Inactive foundation.

name: STAUNTON FARM FOUNDATION
reasoning: Staunton Farm Foundation funds behavioral health and mental health programs exclusively in Western Pennsylvania (10-county region). VetsBoats is in San Francisco Bay Area. While the Foundation has funded some vet mental health work, the strict geographic restriction to Western PA is disqualifying.

name: SUTAR-SUTARUK-MEYER FOUNDATION
reasoning: The foundation's website redirected to Cybo (a Latvian business directory), yielding no application or eligibility information. Grant history shows veteran assistance grants primarily to Massachusetts shelters and social services — not adaptive recreation. No application process identifiable.

name: THE AHMANSON FOUNDATION
reasoning: The Ahmanson Foundation's eligibility page explicitly states: 'The Foundation reviews funding requests from 501(c)(3) public charity organizations... that are not private foundations as defined in sections 509(a)(1) or 509(a)(2) of the U.S. Internal Revenue Code.' VetsBoats is a private foundation — this is a direct, explicit disqualification.

name: THE AMATURO FAMILY FOUNDATION
reasoning: The foundation's website redirected to GuideStar/Candid, yielding no application or eligibility information. Grant history shows small veteran gifts ($1,000-$14,000) channeled through United Way of Broward County (FL) — suggesting a Florida-focused, donor-directed approach with no open application process.

name: THE AMBROSE MONELL FOUNDATION
reasoning: The Ambrose Monell Foundation explicitly states on their website: 'As of March 1, 2021, the Foundation will no longer be accepting unsolicited letters of inquiry (LOI) or proposals. All grants will be initiated internally by the Foundation.' Despite interesting vet grants (Higher Ground adaptive sports program), they are invitation-only with no application process.

name: THE ANNE AND HENRY ZARROW FOUNDATION
reasoning: Zarrow Foundation is Tulsa-focused (homelessness, affordable housing, Tulsa community needs). Their vet grants are exclusively for the Tulsa VA hospital project. No adaptive recreation mission overlap and strong geographic concentration in Oklahoma.

name: THE BAMFORD FOUNDATION
reasoning: Bamford Foundation explicitly serves Tacoma, Washington and the South Puget Sound area. VetsBoats is in San Francisco Bay Area. Their vet grants are to Pierce College's veteran support center in Tacoma. Geographic exclusivity is disqualifying.

name: THE BANK OF AMERICA CHARITABLE
reasoning: Bank of America Charitable Foundation is a corporate foundation that directs grants through BofA's internal community affairs processes — not open to unsolicited applications from external nonprofits, let alone private foundations. Zero vet grants in their history and website shows only consumer banking content.
